visible_agent_inputs.question: United 300 and 300 were both made in what country?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1: Evidence: the passages state "The Farman F.300 and F.310 were airliners built in France in the early 1930s" and "The Morane‑Saulnier MS.300 ... were French parasol wing introductory trainer aircraft." Missing/ambiguous link: none of the provided passages mention "United 300" or specify which two "300" items the question means. Retrieval direction: fetch the page(s) for "United 300" and the relevant "300" disambiguation entries (or the specific "300" article the question refers to) to identify those two named items and confirm their country of manufacture. Use the quoted anchors "built in France" and "French ... trainer aircraft" when matching candidate "300" articles.

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Community Unit School District 300 (disambiguation) | Community Unit School District 300 (disambiguation)
300 (disambiguation) | 300 is a year, the year 300 CE. 300 may also refer to:

No. 300 Polish Bomber Squadron | No. 300 (Polish) "Land of Masovia" Bomber Squadron (Polish: "300 Dywizjon Bombowy "Ziemi Mazowieckiej"" ) was a Polish World War II bomber unit. It was fighting alongside the Royal Air Force and operated from airfields in the United Kingdom.

LR-300 | The LR 300 is an American rifle and conversion kit designed by gunsmith Allan Zitta and manufactured by Z-M Weapons. The "LR" stands for "Light Rifle" and 300 is for 300 meters, the effective range of the rifle with a standard 55-grain FMJ bullet. It is based on the AR-15 and is broadly similar to the United States service rifle, the M16.
Alsco 300 | The Alsco 300 is a NASCAR Xfinity Series race held at Kentucky Speedway in Sparta, Kentucky, United States. The distance of the race is 300 miles (482.803 km).

.300 Norma Magnum | The .300 Norma Magnum is a cartridge that has begun to gain popularity in the long range shooting community. The .300 Norma Magnum is a .338 Norma Magnum case necked down to .308 caliber. This cartridge was named .300 Norma Magnum to differentiate it from the .308 Norma Magnum designed in 1960. The United States Special Operations Command (SOCOM) has selected .300 Norma Magnum as their new Advanced Sniper Rifle Cartridge (2016).

KTM 300 | The KTM 300 is a series of 2-stroke enduro off-road motorcycles made by KTM. They are the Penton-inspired 1984 300 GS, the 300 DMX, the dirt only 300 MXC, European road legal 300 EXC and their current US counterparts the 300 XC (close-ratio) and 300 XC-W (wide-ratio gear box) respectively. The KTM 300 is designed for difficult off-road conditions. The 300 EXC and the 300 XC-W have a small headlight, speedometer, tail-light and somewhat softer linkless PDS suspension. Starting in 2009 a ...[truncated]

The retrieved passages do not identify or link an entity called "United 300," so whether "United 300" refers to one of these or was also made in France is not confirmed by the search results.

output.query: 300 (film) United 300 United 300 and 300 were both made in what country?

retrieved_doc_snippets: United 300 | United 300 is an American short film that parodies "United 93" (2006) and "300" (2007). It won the MTV Movie Spoof Award at the MTV Movie Awards 2007. The short was created by Andy Signore along with some of his friends. During his speech, he said, roughly, "The film was not making fun of a tragedy; it was a tribute to those who stood up against tyranny".

KTM 300 | The KTM 300 is a series of 2-stroke enduro off-road motorcycles made by KTM. They are the Penton-inspired 1984 300 GS, the 300 DMX, the dirt only 300 MXC, European road legal 300 EXC and their current US counterparts the 300 XC (close-ratio) and 300 XC-W (wide-ratio gear box) respectively. The KTM 300 is designed for difficult off-road conditions. The 300 EXC and the 300 XC-W have a small headlight, speedometer, tail-light and somewhat softer linkless PDS suspension. Starting in 2009 a ...[truncated]

Fortschritt ZT 300 | ZT 300 is a series of 20-kN-tractors produced from 1967 to 1984 by the VEB Traktorenwerk Schönebeck. It succeeded the "RS14 Famulus" series and unlike the Famulus, the ZT 300 series was sold under the brand name Fortschritt. "ZT 300" stands for the ZT 300 series tractors as well as the initial tractor Fortschritt ZT 300. In total, 72.382 tractors were made. The most built model was the ZT 303 introduced in 1972 with automatic all-wheel-drive; in the early 1980s it would cost ...[truncated]
British Rail Class 300 | Class 300 was allocated to a proposed fleet of parcels-carrying electric multiple units, which were to be converted from former Class 307 units.

.300 Remington Short Action Ultra Magnum | .300 Remington Short Action Ultra Magnum (also known as 300 RSAUM, 300 RSUM or 300 Rem SAUM) is a .30 caliber short magnum cartridge that is a shortened version of the Remington 300 Ultra Mag, both of which derive from the .404 Jeffery case. The Remington Short Ultra Mag was put on the market shortly after Winchester released its 300 WSM round in 2001, resulting in the Winchester product getting the marketing advantage that has eclipsed the Remington of ...[truncated]

No. 300 Polish Bomber Squadron | No. 300 (Polish) "Land of Masovia" Bomber Squadron (Polish: "300 Dywizjon Bombowy "Ziemi Mazowieckiej"" ) was a Polish World War II bomber unit. It was fighting alongside the Royal Air Force and operated from airfields in the United Kingdom.

300 (film) | 300 is a 2006 American epic war film based on the 1998 comic series "300" by Frank Miller and Lynn Varley. Both are fictionalized retellings of the Battle of Thermopylae within the Persian Wars. The film was directed by Zack Snyder, while Miller served as executive producer and consultant. It was filmed mostly with a super-imposition chroma key technique, to help replicate the imagery of the original comic book.
target_state.summary_2: Both United 300 and 300 are American films — they were made in the United States.
